torch_pattern: reshaped = x.reshape(N, H // stride_h, stride_h, W // stride_w, stride_w, C); transposed = reshaped.permute(0, 1, 3, 2, 4, 5); out = transposed.reshape(N, H // stride_h, W // stride_w, C * stride_h * stride_w)
applicability_notes: Replaces the reshape+permute+reshape (space-to-depth / patchify) subsequence; commonly fused ahead of a linear projection (ViT patch embedding: reshape -> fold -> to_layout(TILE) -> linear) or as the unfold stage of a large-kernel stride conv (ResNet stem). Input must be ROW_MAJOR NHWC. Channel padding to a multiple of 4 may be required: padding[5] = nearest_y(C,4) - C.; constraints: {'input_layout': 'ROW_MAJOR_LAYOUT', 'input_format': 'NHWC', 'stride_divisibility': 'H % stride_h == 0 and W % stride_w == 0 (after padding)'}

placement_observations.source: unit_test: tt_input_tensor = ttnn.from_torch(..., memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG); ttnn.fold(tt_input_tensor, stride_h, stride_w, padding=list(padding))
